hero.headline: Portfolio Evaluation Against 100,000+ Peer Allocations
hero.subheadline: AI-powered audit benchmarks your allocation against institutional-grade data in 60 seconds
hero.body: Compare your portfolio allocation, diversification, and risk exposure against UHNW peer benchmarks across 50+ markets. Identify systematic gaps in alternative exposure, geographic concentration, and vintage diversification using institutional audit frameworks.

sections.title: Institutional Audit Framework
sections.content:
Our evaluation engine applies the same allocation analysis framework used by family offices managing $500M+ portfolios. The system benchmarks seven dimensions: asset class diversification (target: 15-25% alternatives per UBS Global Family Office Report 2026), geographic allocation (optimal: 40-60% non-domestic per Cambridge Associates), vintage year distribution (ideal: 3-5 year rolling commitments), manager concentration (risk threshold: max 15% single GP), sector exposure (recommended: 8-12 sectors minimum per Preqin Standards), liquidity reserves (benchmark: 2-3 years operating capital), and tax efficiency (target: sub-30% effective rate for UHNW structures per PwC Global Wealth Tax Guide).

The audit identifies three categories of variance from institutional norms: structural gaps (missing asset classes, under-allocated alternatives), concentration risks (over-exposure to single managers, geographies, or vintages), and optimization opportunities (tax-inefficient structures, illiquidity mismatches). Each dimension receives a percentile ranking against our database of 100,000+ verified UHNW portfolios managing $50B+ in aggregate capital (data: 2023-2026, refreshed quarterly).

Analysis completes in 60 seconds using machine learning models trained on institutional allocation patterns, returning a 12-page diagnostic with specific gap metrics, peer percentile rankings, and quantified opportunity estimates.

sections.title: UHNW Peer Allocation Benchmarks
sections.content:
Institutional portfolios managing $10M+ demonstrate consistent allocation patterns across our database. Median alternative exposure: 23% of total portfolio (range: 18-32% for $10-50M portfolios, 28-38% for $50M+ per Knight Frank Wealth Report 2026). Private equity comprises 35-45% of alternatives allocation, with venture capital at 15-25%, private credit 20-30%, and real assets 10-15% (Source: Preqin Investor Benchmarking Report Q4 2025).

Geographic diversification averages 48% non-domestic exposure for US-based UHNW investors, with 25-35% in developed international markets and 8-15% in emerging markets (Source: Cambridge Associates Global Portfolio Construction Study 2025). Asia-Pacific allocation increased from 12% (2023) to 17% (2026) among family offices managing $100M+ (Source: UBS Global Family Office Report 2026).

Vintage year distribution for top-quartile portfolios maintains 3-5 active commitment years simultaneously, limiting single-vintage concentration to maximum 35% of private markets allocation (Source: Hamilton Lane Vintage Year Diversification Study 2025). Manager concentration stays below 12-15% per individual GP for portfolios exceeding $25M (Source: Institutional Limited Partners Association Best Practices 2025).
sections.image_query: wealth management office with financial charts

sections.title: Systematic Gap Identification
sections.content:
The audit quantifies three categories of allocation variance. Structural gaps identify missing or under-weighted asset classes relative to institutional benchmarks: portfolios below 15% alternative exposure (current peer median: 23%) represent 8-10% annual return opportunity cost based on 10-year private equity PME of 1.35x vs public markets (Source: Cambridge Associates PE Index 2025). Geographic concentration above 60% single-country exposure increases volatility by 15-22% vs diversified peers (Source: MSCI Global Portfolio Risk Analysis 2025).

Concentration risks flag over-exposure to individual managers, sectors, or vintages. Single manager exposure exceeding 15% of alternatives portfolio increases idiosyncratic risk by 25-35% (Source: Allocation Advisors Risk Measurement Study 2024). Sector concentration above 30% in technology or single sectors amplifies drawdown risk by 18-28% during market corrections (Source: Pitchbook Sector Concentration Analysis 2025).

Optimization opportunities identify tax-inefficient structures, liquidity mismatches, and fee compression potential. Portfolios using suboptimal tax vehicles incur 5-8% annual drag vs properly structured alternatives (Source: EY Global Wealth Tax Efficiency Report 2026). Excess liquidity beyond 2-3 years operating reserves represents 2-4% opportunity cost vs deployed capital (Source: Northern Trust Liquidity Management Study 2025).
sections.image_query: executive analyzing financial data on laptop

sections.title: Quantified Opportunity Mapping
sections.content:
Each identified gap receives a quantified opportunity estimate based on historical peer performance data. Under-allocated alternatives: portfolios moving from 10% to 23% alternative exposure (peer median) historically achieved 1.8-2.4% incremental annual return (10-year data, risk-adjusted) per Cambridge Associates transition analysis. Geographic diversification: reducing single-country concentration from 70% to 50% decreased portfolio volatility by 12-18% while maintaining equivalent returns (Source: MSCI Geographic Diversification Study 2023-2026).

Vintage diversification improvements: spreading commitments across 4-5 years vs 1-2 year concentration reduced peak-to-trough drawdowns by 15-25% during market cycles (Source: Hamilton Lane Vintage Analysis 2015-2025). Manager diversification: reducing top-3 manager concentration from 50% to 35% of alternatives improved risk-adjusted returns by 0.8-1.2% annually (Sharpe ratio improvement: 0.15-0.22 points) per Allocation Advisors database.

Tax optimization: restructuring to institutional-grade vehicles typically recovers 3-6% annual after-tax return on alternatives portfolio through proper use of blocker corporations, offshore structures, and tax-deferred exchanges (Source: PwC UHNW Tax Optimization Case Studies 2025). Total quantified opportunity across all gaps: 4-9% incremental annual return potential for portfolios with multiple structural deficiencies vs peer benchmarks.
